response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine distributed-systems depth from his Dunnhumby tenure (millions of HTTP requests per second, low-latency async I/O) and his current re:cinq role directly touches AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which aligns well with the AI-native engineering baseline. However, Python — listed as a hard must-have — is completely absent from his resume, skills list, and project history, which is a material gap for this role. His Staff-level titles are real but the breadth of technical-strategy ownership and cross-functional stakeholder coordination is not well evidenced. He warrants a screening call to clarify the Python situation and the scope of his current AI infrastructure work before a firm decision.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP req/s, low-latency async I/O in production)
Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework development, satisfying the AI-native engineering baseline
Demonstrated ownership mindset across career: took over iOS development solo, founded and maintained GISCollective long-term, held Staff titles at multiple orgs
concerns: Python is entirely absent from skills, work history, and open-source projects — a stated must-have with no signal it can be remediated quickly
Distributed systems depth is concentrated in 2018–2020; post-2020 work (pricing optimisation, direct mail, tooling) does not demonstrate continued high-throughput infrastructure ownership
No visible evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, incident response / on-call ownership, or technical strategy influence at org or domain scope
